text.title: OpenAPI Spec Authoring Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every api-openapi-spec artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Spec MUST lint clean against `templates/spectral.yaml` ruleset (no errors; warnings tracked). PRs with new spec errors are blocked.
rule.rationale: Linting prevents the same five-mistake recurrences across every endpoint; ruleset is repository policy made executable.

rule.statement: Schemas referenced by >1 operation MUST live under components.schemas and be referenced by $ref. Duplicate inline schemas are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Inline duplicates drift; one is updated, the other is not, and the docs lie about half the endpoints.

rule.statement: Every documented response status MUST have a typed schema; freeform responses (`{}` or `type: object` without properties) are forbidden.

rationale.source: OpenAPI Initiative + JSON Schema strictness guidance

rule.rationale: Freeform schemas defeat codegen and let backends add fields without contract.

rule.statement: info.x-versioning MUST declare versioning_scheme (url-path / accept-header / header-key) + breaking-change policy + deprecation window. Undeclared versioning is forbidden.

rationale.source: Internal — API-versioning audit + Stripe / GitHub versioning guidance

rule.rationale: Without a declared scheme, every endpoint can version independently; clients cannot predict deprecation.

rule.statement: components.securitySchemes MUST match the AUTH-* artefact (scheme name + flows + scopes). Drift between spec and runtime auth is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mismatch between docs and runtime makes clients fail with confusing 401s; partners blame docs first.
